Package org.eventb.internal.ui.preferences

Class AbstractFieldPreferenceAndPropertyPage

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.jface.dialogs.DialogPage
org.eclipse.jface.preference.PreferencePage
org.eventb.internal.ui.preferences.AbstractEventBPreferencePage
org.eventb.internal.ui.preferences.AbstractFieldPreferenceAndPropertyPage
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.jface.dialogs.IDialogPage, org.eclipse.jface.dialogs.IMessageProvider, org.eclipse.jface.preference.IPreferencePage, org.eclipse.ui.IWorkbenchPreferencePage, org.eclipse.ui.IWorkbenchPropertyPage
Direct Known Subclasses:
PostAutoTacticPreferencePage, PrefixPreferencePage
public abstract class AbstractFieldPreferenceAndPropertyPage extends AbstractEventBPreferencePage implements org.eclipse.ui.IWorkbenchPropertyPage, org.eclipse.ui.IWorkbenchPreferencePage
Class used to create a page for both workbench preferences and project relative properties page. It uses a scoped store on the concerned project that save the properties. This class is intended to be sub-classed.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class org.eventb.internal.ui.preferences.AbstractEventBPreferencePage

    AbstractEventBPreferencePage.BooleanEventBEditor, AbstractEventBPreferencePage.EventBFieldEditor, AbstractEventBPreferencePage.StringEventBEditor

    • isPropertyPage

      public boolean isPropertyPage()
      Returns true if this instance represents a property page (i.e. a project is set as owner).
      Returns:
      true for property pages, false for preference pages

    • showPropertiesPage

      public void showPropertiesPage(String id, org.eclipse.jface.preference.IPreferencePage page, org.eclipse.core.resources.IProject prj)
      Show a single preference page for a project (i.e. property page)
      Parameters:
      id - the preference page identification
      page - the preference page

    • initializeDefaultProperties

      protected abstract void initializeDefaultProperties()
      Method that users should implement, in order to load the values of the project scope preference store with the workspace global values. The user is responsible of the preferences types he wants to use. The user should implement the way that workspace preference values can overwrite the project specific values in case of a defaults restoration when reset is set to true.

    • getWorkspacePreferencePage

      protected AbstractFieldPreferenceAndPropertyPage getWorkspacePreferencePage()
      Returns the workspace preference page which opened this page.

      If the preference page is not a project specific preference page or is a properties page, return null.
